response.justification: The candidate brings nearly two decades of broad engineering experience, current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and proven high-throughput distributed systems work (micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby). However, Python — a must-have — is absent from his skill list and work history, his highest-throughput experience is in DLang and dates to 2020, and evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, and operational/on-call ownership is thin on the resume. Strong craftsmanship and AI-native exposure make him worth a conversation, but the gaps temper the fit.
strengths: Current AI-native experience: builds MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks
Real high-throughput distributed systems background (millions of HTTP requests/sec, low-latency async I/O)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling author and educator
concerns: No Python listed anywhere despite it being a must-have skill
Limited evidence of staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or driving decisions at scale
No stated on-call/incident-response experience; deepest systems work is in DLang and several years old
